use std::fs;
use std::path::PathBuf;

#[test]
fn test_write_report() {
    let path = std::env::temp_dir().join("test_report.txt");
    fs::write(&path, "report data").unwrap();
    assert!(path.exists());
}

#[test]
fn test_read_log() {
    let path = std::env::temp_dir().join("test_log.log");
    fs::write(&path, "log entry 1\nlog entry 2").unwrap();
    let content = fs::read_to_string(&path).unwrap();
    assert!(content.contains("log entry"));
}

#[test]
fn test_config_file() {
    let path = std::env::temp_dir().join("test_config.toml");
    fs::write(&path, "[server]\nport = 8080").unwrap();
    let _ = fs::remove_file(&path);
}

#[test]
fn test_output_file() {
    let path = PathBuf::from("/tmp/test_output.json");
    fs::write(&path, r#"{"status": "ok"}"#).unwrap();
    assert!(path.exists());
    let _ = fs::remove_file(&path);
}
